text: Convention treated as hard invariant — no enforcement path for workbench MUST-update obligation across 4 platforms
§3.5 staleness detection not implementable — memory_list() returns no timestamps per namespace
MAX(updated_at) misses delete activity (rows gone) and read activity — proposes multi-field activity tracking
Read-time staleness implies write-on-read side effects — handshake reads become mutating operations
Workbench as single orientation read is a single point of misinformation if stale
Concurrent updates to meta/workbench will lose data — no compare-and-swap on memory_write
Tiered handshake Tier 3 trigger requires cross-session persistent memory that Claude doesn't have
Convention-only replacement drops hard guarantees — no token budget, no session lifecycle, no disconnect-driven closure
meta/last-session at <40% write reliability becomes low-trust noise that agents learn to ignore
Markdown is a fragile machine contract — formatting drift breaks cross-session parsing
14-day staleness threshold and 300-word workbench target are arbitrary and will conflict at scale
Strict markdown template dropped Conventions version field, breaking Tier 3 trigger logic
Ad-hoc 7-day workbench staleness trigger introduced while threshold policy was deferred — internally inconsistent
